title: GetResponse vs AWeber (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare GetResponse vs AWeber. See pricing, features, ratings & which email marketing tool wins for your needs.
intro: Both GetResponse and AWeber are veteran email marketing platforms founded in 1998, offering freemium models with 500 free subscribers. GetResponse positions itself as a complete marketing suite with webinars and courses, while AWeber emphasizes simplicity for bloggers and solopreneurs. Your choice depends on whether you need advanced automation features or straightforward email marketing.
choose_a_if: You host webinars or sell online courses and need integrated tools
You want advanced conversion funnels and sales automation
You need a comprehensive all-in-one marketing platform beyond email
choose_b_if: You prioritize simplicity and ease of use for email marketing
You're a blogger wanting RSS-to-email functionality
You prefer lower pricing and better Trustpilot reviews

faq.a: GetResponse is better if you need advanced features like webinars, online courses, and conversion funnels. AWeber is better if you want simplicity and lower cost. Both scored 4.2/5 on G2, but AWeber has a higher Trustpilot rating (4.0 vs 3.7), suggesting better customer satisfaction with their core email service.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, GetResponse or AWeber?
faq.a: AWeber is $4/month cheaper at the entry level ($15 vs $19). Both offer identical free tiers with 500 subscribers. However, GetResponse's extra features may justify the higher price depending on your needs.

faq.q: Can I switch from AWeber to GetResponse?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support importing contacts from other email services. GetResponse provides tools to migrate your subscriber lists, automation sequences, and templates. The process is straightforward, though you should test your campaigns after migration.
